# Test JSON scanning functionality in mcpspy
#
# This script tests the mcpspy tool's ability to parse and format JSON messages
# from stdin and log them properly with timestamps.

# Basic JSON processing test
cat input.json | mcpspy -f output1.log -v -- echo "test"
! stderr "error"
# Verify that all JSON objects were captured
grep "mcp-recv" output1.log
stdout "test"

# Test pretty-print JSON option
cat input.json | mcpspy -f output2.log -v -pretty -- echo "test pretty"
! stderr "error"
grep "mcp-recv" output2.log
stdout "test pretty"

# Test with split/fragmented JSON (simulate slow input)
cat split_json.txt | mcpspy -f output3.log -v -- echo "test split"
! stderr "error"
grep "mcp-recv" output3.log
stdout "test split"

# Test basic pipe mode
echo '{"method": "test"}' | mcpspy -f output4.log -v -pipe
grep "mcp-recv" output4.log

# Data files
-- input.json --
{"method": "initialize", "params": {"protocolVersion": "2024-11-05", "clientInfo": {"name": "test-client", "version": "1.0.0"}, "capabilities": {}}}
{"method": "tools/list", "params": {}}
{"method": "tools/call", "params": {"name": "TestTool", "arguments": {"key": "value"}}}

-- split_json.txt --
{"method": "split_test", "param
": "value"}
